title: Spatial Topology
description: Display → Window topology for agent-os. Produced by `aos see list`, consumed by `aos do` and orchestrators. Display and cursor bounds are emitted in both native desktop compatibility coordinates (for AppKit/CG boundary consumers) and DesktopWorld coordinates (canonical cross-surface world). Cursor DesktopWorld siblings live on this topology schema only — they are not part of the `display_geometry` channel payload. See `shared/schemas/spatial-topology.md`.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this snapshot was taken.

screens_have_separate_spaces.description: NSScreen.screensHaveSeparateSpaces. true = each display has independent spaces (default macOS). false = all displays share one space set.

cursor.description: Current mouse cursor position in current producer coordinates (native desktop compatibility points).

focused_window_id.description: CGWindowID of the focused window. Null if no window is focused.

focused_app.description: The frontmost application. Null if none.

displays.description: All active displays, ordered: main first, then left-to-right by X coordinate.

desktop_world_bounds.description: Full DesktopWorld union bounds. Origin is (0,0) by construction; width/height span the arranged full-display union.

visible_desktop_world_bounds.description: Union of each display's visible_bounds projected into DesktopWorld. Use for usable-area logic (cursor/avatar clamping).

apps.description: All running GUI applications with at least one window.

x.description: Cursor X in native desktop compatibility coordinates.

y.description: Cursor Y in native desktop compatibility coordinates.

desktop_world_x.description: Cursor X in DesktopWorld coordinates (canonical cross-surface world).

display.description: Ordinal of the display the cursor is on.

x.description: Left edge in native desktop compatibility coordinates (retained for compat; prefer native_bounds / desktop_world_bounds on Display).

y.description: Top edge in native desktop compatibility coordinates.

x.description: Left edge in native desktop compatibility coordinates (top-left of macOS main display = (0,0)).

y.description: Top edge in native desktop compatibility coordinates.

x.description: Left edge in DesktopWorld coordinates (top-left of arranged full-display union = (0,0)).

display_id.description: CGDirectDisplayID. Session-scoped but stable across sleep/wake.

display_uuid.description: CGDisplayCreateUUIDFromDisplayID. Persistent across reboots. Null if unavailable.

bounds.description: Full display frame in native desktop compatibility coordinates. Kept for AppKit/CG boundary consumers; shared-world consumers should prefer desktop_world_bounds.

visible_bounds.description: Usable area excluding menu bar and dock (NSScreen.visibleFrame), in native desktop compatibility coordinates.

native_bounds.description: Explicit alias of bounds for native-boundary consumers.

native_visible_bounds.description: Explicit alias of visible_bounds for native-boundary consumers.

desktop_world_bounds.description: Full display frame in DesktopWorld coordinates. Canonical cross-surface value.

visible_desktop_world_bounds.description: Usable area in DesktopWorld coordinates (VisibleDesktopWorld projection of visible_bounds).

windows.description: Visible windows on this display, ordered front-to-back (index 0 = frontmost).

title.description: Window title from CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo. Null if untitled.

bundle_id.description: Owning app CFBundleIdentifier. Null for unbundled processes.

bounds.description: Window frame in current producer coordinates (native desktop compatibility points).

is_focused.description: true if this is the focused window.

is_on_screen.description: true if the window is currently visible (not minimized, not on another space).

is_active.description: true if this is the frontmost application.

is_hidden.description: true if the app is hidden (Cmd+H).

window_ids.description: All CGWindowIDs owned by this app (across all displays).
